item.title: Why electrified drops resist splashing
item.description: Electrified drops do not splash upon impacting a surface because the electrostatic charge creates an attraction between the drop and the surface, which suppresses the outward spreading that creates a splash.

media:content.media:title: Why electrified drops resist splashing
media:content.media:description: Electrified drops do not splash upon impacting a surface because the electrostatic charge creates an attraction between the drop and the surface, which suppresses the outward spreading that creates a splash.

item.title: Shining IR light through myself
item.description: Infrared light can penetrate the body two to three times as much as regular light. So, let's see if I can shine it through myself.

media:content.media:title: Shining IR light through myself
media:content.media:description: Infrared light can penetrate the body two to three times as much as regular light. So, let's see if I can shine it through myself.

item.title: Aerogel protects me from lava
item.description: Aerogel, a solid material composed of over 98% air, acts as an incredibly efficient thermal insulator that protects against molten lava

media:content.media:title: Aerogel protects me from lava
media:content.media:description: Aerogel, a solid material composed of over 98% air, acts as an incredibly efficient thermal insulator that protects against molten lava

item.title: Making light come out of my eyes!
item.description: In this video I shine the world’s brightest flashlight (32,000 lumen) into my mouth in order to attempt to shoot light out of my eyes! This crazy experiment shows you how you can shine light through the human body. I show you how you can change the color of light through the body to be, not just red, but yellow as well by using my sodium vapor lamp. The I tell you about Know Labs, a company that is making the world’s first wearable real-time blood-glucose monitoring device! I teach you about how a device like this works using readio-frequencies.

media:content.media:title: Making light come out of my eyes!
media:content.media:description: In this video I shine the world’s brightest flashlight (32,000 lumen) into my mouth in order to attempt to shoot light out of my eyes! This crazy experiment shows you how you can shine light through the human body. I show you how you can change the color of light through the body to be, not just red, but yellow as well by using my sodium vapor lamp. The I tell you about Know Labs, a company that is making the world’s first wearable real-time blood-glucose monitoring device! I teach you about how a device like this works using readio-frequencies.

item.title: Magnetic Face Mask vs. Neodymium Magnet
item.description: How a magnetic face mask reacts to a giant neodymium magnet in this science experiment.

media:content.media:title: Magnetic Face Mask vs. Neodymium Magnet
media:content.media:description: How a magnetic face mask reacts to a giant neodymium magnet in this science experiment.

item.title: Negative kelvin temperature!
item.description: In this video I show you what it means to have negative temperature by focusing a laser beam down to a single point. I show you what happens if you try to focus a light down to a single point, then I show you how a laser is different due to population inversion.

media:content.media:title: Negative kelvin temperature!
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you what it means to have negative temperature by focusing a laser beam down to a single point. I show you what happens if you try to focus a light down to a single point, then I show you how a laser is different due to population inversion.

item.title: Sticking my finger in a gallium vortex
item.description: I show you how to make a gallium vortex

media:content.media:title: Sticking my finger in a gallium vortex
media:content.media:description: I show you how to make a gallium vortex
